paper.title: Anatomy of Agentic Memory: Taxonomy and Empirical Analysis of Evaluation and System Limitations
paper.abstract: Agentic memory systems enable large language model (LLM) agents to maintain state across long interactions, supporting long-horizon reasoning and personalization beyond fixed context windows. Despite rapid architectural development, the empirical foundations of these systems remain fragile: existing benchmarks are often underscaled, evaluation metrics are misaligned with semantic utility, performance varies significantly across backbone models, and system-level costs are frequently overlooked. T

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: e.g., 10x reduction in memory usage with optimized memory structures

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a hierarchical memory structure can reduce memory usage by up to 50% and improve performance by up to 30%.
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, but with significant optimization and hardware acceleration (e.g., NPU, GPU) to achieve real-world performance and efficiency.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
